benchmark_details.name: DAHL (Domain-specific Automated Hallucination Evaluation of Long-Form Text through a Benchmark Dataset in Biomedicine)
benchmark_details.overview: DAHL is a benchmark dataset and automated evaluation system designed to assess hallucination in long-form text generation, specifically within the biomedical domain. The dataset consists of 8,573 questions covering 29 categories, evaluating fact-conflicting hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs).

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate the hallucination of LLMs in the biomedical domain by measuring the factuality of long-form responses.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: DAHL primarily targets fact-conflicting hallucination but acknowledges the importance of other types of hallucination.

data.source: PubMed Central (PMC), a freely accessible full-text archive of biomedical and life sciences journal literature.
data.annotation: Questions were generated using gpt-4 and filtered manually for quality.

metrics: DAHL Score based on factual accuracy
methodology.calculation: Factual precision is calculated by dividing the number of accurate atomic units by the total number of atomic units derived from a response.
methodology.interpretation: Higher DAHL Scores indicate better factual accuracy in the responses generated by LLMs.
methodology.baseline_results: DAHL Scores for various models tested: gpt-4o (0.9365), Qwen-2 (0.7610 for 20.5B params).
methodology.validation: DAHL Score was validated through comparison with human evaluation results.
